title: Isolating data for analysis to avoid malicious attacks
abstract: Determining, by a machine learning model in an isolated operating environment, whether a file is safe for processing by a primary operating environment. The file is provided, when the determining indicates the file is safe for processing, to the primary operating environment for processing by the primary operating environment. When the determining indicates the file is unsafe for processing, the file is prevented from being processed by the primary operating environment. The isolated operating environment can be maintained on an isolated computing system remote from a primary computing system maintaining the primary operating system. The isolating computing system and the primary operating system can communicate over a cloud network.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: determining, by a machine learning model in an isolated operating environment, whether a file is safe for processing by a primary operating environment; providing, when the determining indicates the file is safe for processing, the file to the primary operating environment for processing by the primary operating environment; and, preventing, when the determining indicates the file is unsafe for processing, the file from being processed by the primary operating environment. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the determining is performed in response to an indication that the file has an unsafe file type. 3. The method of any of claims 1-2, wherein the isolated operating environment and the primary operating environment are hosted by the same machine. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, wherein the isolated operating environment is hosted by an isolated machine remote from a primary machine which is hosting the primary operating environment. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, further comprising transmitting the file from the primary machine to the isolated machine prior to the determining. 6. The method of any of claims 1-5, wherein the isolated machine is connected to the primary machine through a cloud-based network. 7. The method of any of claims 1-6, wherein the determining is performed during loading of an application in the primary operating environment. 8 The method of any of claims 1-7, wherein the application is loaded in the primary operating environment in response to a user request to open the file. 9. The method of any of claims 1-8, wherein the file is an executable-type file. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, wherein the file is a file-type which when interacted with causes the at least one processor to initialize a program to execute the file. 11. The method of any of claims 1-10, wherein the file-type of the file is PE, OLE, MachO, PDF, ELF, JAVA, DOCX or a script. 12. The method of any of claims 1-11, further comprising: monitoring a network to identify files having an unsafe file type transmitted on the network; and providing the file to the isolated operating environment in response to identifying a file having an unsafe file type. 13. The method of any of claims 1-12, wherein the determining is performed in response to a user request to interact with the file. 14. The method of any of claims 1-13, wherein the file includes an email and the determining is performed in response to receipt of an email over a network. 15. The method of any of claims 1-14, wherein the determining is performed in response to a scan of a database indicating that a file stored in the database has an unsafe file type. 16. A system comprising: a processor; and, a memory storing machine-readable instructions, which when executed by the processor, cause the processor to perform one or more operations, the operations comprising: determining, by a machine learning model in an isolated operating environment, whether a file is safe for processing by a primary operating environment; providing, when the determining indicates the file is safe for processing, the file to the primary operating environment for processing by the primary operating environment; and, preventing, when the determining indicates the file is unsafe for processing, the file from being processed by the primary operating environment. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the determining is performed in response to an indication that the file has an unsafe file type. 18. The system of any of claims 16-17, wherein the isolated operating environment and the primary operating environment are hosted by the same machine. 19. The system of any of claims 16-18, wherein the isolated operating environment is hosted by an isolated machine remote from a primary machine which is hosting the primary operating environment. 20. The system of any of claims 16-19, further comprising transmitting the file from the primary machine to the isolated machine prior to the determining. 21. The system of any of claims 16-20, wherein the isolated machine is connected to the primary machine through a cloud-based network. 22. The system of any of claims 16-21, wherein the determining is performed during loading of an application in the primary operating environment. 23. The system of any of claims 16-22, wherein the application is loaded in the primary operating environment in response to a user request to open the file. 24. The system of any of claims 16-23, wherein the file is an executable-type file. 25. The system of any of claims 16-24, wherein the file is a file-type which when interacted with causes the at least one processor to initialize a program to execute the file. 26. The system of any of claims 16-25, wherein the file-type of the file is PE, OLE, MachO, PDF, ELF, JAVA, DOCX or a script. 27. The system of any of claims 16-26, wherein the operations further comprise: : monitoring a network to identify files having an unsafe file type transmitted on the network; and 

 providing the tile to the isolated operating environment in response to identifying a file having an unsafe file type. 28. The system of any of claims 16-27, wherein the determining is performed in response to a user request to interact with the file. 29. The system any of claims 16-28, wherein the file includes an email and the determining is performed in response to receipt of an email over a network. 30. The system of any of claims 16-29, wherein the determining is performed in response to a scan of a database indicating that a file stored in the database has an unsafe file type. 31. An apparatus comprising: means for determining, by a machine learning model in an isolated operating environment, whether a file is safe for processing by a primary operating environment; means for providing, when the determining indicates the file is safe for processing, the file to the primary operating environment for processing by the primary operating environment; and, means for preventing, when the determining indicates the file is unsafe for processing, the file from being processed by the primary operating environment. 32. The apparatus of claim 31, further comprising means for performing any of claims 2-15. 33. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including program code which when executed by at least one processor operations comprising: determining, by a machine learning model in an isolated operating environment, whether a file is safe for processing by a primary operating environment; providing, when the determining indicates the file is safe for processing, the file to the primary operating environment for processing by the primary operating environment; and, preventing, when the determining indicates the file is unsafe for processing, the file from being processed by the primary operating environment.
